summary: Verified the new call -> hook attribution feature end-to-end. Backend pytest (7/7) covers Scenario A (gclid matches a seeded click with browser UA -> saw_landing_page=true, hook_label present, hook1/hook2/matched_rule_id keys present), Scenario B (no matching click -> saw_landing_page=false, hook_label/hook1/hook2/matched_rule_id all null), /admin/calls/test seeding, mark-as-sold revenue passback regression, and auth enforcement (webhook token + admin auth). Frontend Playwright covers: Calls tab label format 'Calls (N)' (data-testid=admin-tab-calls), 'Test call' button adds a row, the new 'Hook seen' column renders the gray 'No page visit' badge (data-testid=call-nopage-<id>) for unmatched test calls, View opens the detail dialog with the 'Landing page & hook' section showing the no-page message (data-testid=call-detail-nopage), and the 'Mark as Sold & Send to Google Ads' control (data-testid=call-mark-sold-button) still renders for editable users. All 4 review items PASS.

issues: Console warning: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby for DialogContent' (Radix a11y). Non-blocking but worth adding a DialogDescription for screen readers.

critical_code_review_comments: AdminDashboard.jsx line 251: Calls tab label uses stats?.total_calls which is the lifetime/global stat, not the currently-filtered range that the table shows. When the user filters the Calls tab by a date range, the table can show e.g. 3 calls while the trigger still reads 'Calls (12)'. Either drive the trigger count from the same /admin/calls response the tab loads, or rename the badge for clarity.

_enrich_calls_with_hooks (server.py ~1243): the $or aggregation query is built with empty arrays when both gclids and sessions are empty - the function correctly skips the DB call only when both lists are empty, but if only one is empty an `$or: []` query would be invalid. Currently safe (the if-guard prevents it), but the inner append-only logic could regress; consider building `$or` as a list and only attaching it when non-empty (already done) and assert len($or) > 0 before find.

AdminCalls.jsx convBadge + addTestCall do not optimistically reload stats - the parent stats counter that powers 'Calls (N)' is fetched in AdminDashboard and is not invalidated after add/delete, so the tab count drifts until next full refresh.

calls_webhook silently coerces missing/invalid duration to 0 and accepts any payload shape. Worth at least logging when none of the recognized caller_number aliases are present so misconfigured CTM webhooks surface early.
seed_data_creation: Created scratch calls with caller_name 'QA Hook Test ...' and a few clicks with session_id prefix 'TESTHOOK_<uuid>' during the pytest run. Cleanup fixture deletes the calls it tracked via DELETE /api/admin/calls/{id}. Click documents (a handful) remain in db.clicks with the TESTHOOK_ session prefix and the TESTHOOK_A_* gclids - safe (test gclids will never match a real Google Ads click).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend join is implemented in _enrich_calls_with_hooks (server.py ~L1243). Key gotcha for any future setup: POST /api/track/click drops requests with curl-style/bot User-Agents via is_bot_ua(); always send a real browser UA (e.g. Mozilla/5.0 ... Chrome/120 Safari/537.36) when seeding clicks, otherwise the join correctly returns saw_landing_page=false and you'll think the feature is broken. The hook detail UI uses two mutually-exclusive testids: call-detail-hook-label (with hook1/hook2) when saw_landing_page=true, else call-detail-nopage. The table column uses call-hook-<id> vs call-nopage-<id>. Admin tab trigger format is 'Calls (N)' with data-testid=admin-tab-calls. Note: tab count comes from stats?.total_calls (global), not the currently-filtered table.
rca of the issue: N/A - feature addition, no defect found. Implementation is correct: enrichment loads clicks by gclid (and falls back to session_id), maps matched_rule_id -> hook_rules.label/hook1/hook2, and uses the config default hook when matched_rule_id is null but a click was found. Calls without a matching click are correctly flagged saw_landing_page=false with all hook fields null.
